title: Vanier Institute Conference Funding 2026 – Financial Support for Graduate Students and Postdoctoral Fellows in Family Science
summary: Are you a master s student, doctoral candidate, or postdoctoral fellow working in family science? The Vanier Institute of the Family Conference Funding 2026 program is now open for applications. This funding opportunity is designed to support graduate students and postdoctoral researchers who are actively engaged in family science knowledge exchange and wish to participate in academic conferences. If you are presenting research, sharing findings, or contributing to discussions that advance the understanding of families and social wellbeing, this program can help offset your participation costs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Vanier Institute of the Family The Vanier Institute of the Family is a Canadian organization dedicated to advancing knowledge and research about families. Through research dissemination and knowledge exchange initiatives, the Institute supports evidence-based dialogue that strengthens family wellbeing and policy development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); This conference funding initiative reflects the Institute s commitment to supporting emerging scholars and researchers in the field of family science. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); What the Conference Funding Covers The funding supports participation in conferences related to family science knowledge exchange. While specific amounts and allowable expenses should be confirmed on the official page, support typically contributes toward: Conference registration fees Travel expenses Accommodation costs Knowledge dissemination activities Applicants should carefully review eligible expenses and funding limits before applying. Why You Should Apply Participating in academic conferences provides valuable opportunities to: Present your research to an international audience Expand your academic and professional network Receive feedback from experts in the field Strengthen your CV and academic profile Increase the visibility and impact of your research ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); This funding can reduce financial barriers and help you maximize these professional development opportunities. Who Is Eligible? The funding is available to: Master s students Doctoral students Postdoctoral fellows Applicants must be engaged in family science research and knowledge exchange activities. Application Timeline Applications for the 2026 cycle are now open. Applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official page for specific submission deadlines and funding timelines, as late applications may not be considered. Final Thoughts Conference participation plays a critical role in shaping academic careers, particularly for early-career researchers. If your work contributes to advancing family science, this funding opportunity can help you share your insights, strengthen collaborations, and contribute meaningfully to research-informed dialogue. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); How to Apply Interested applicants should review the detailed eligibility requirements and submit their application via the official link. For more OFY conferences opportunities, Click Here. title: Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students
summary: The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme 2026 offers doctoral students an opportunity to strengthen their PhD research while working with one of the world s leading development economics institutes. This fully funded fellowship allows researchers to access UNU-WIDER s resources, collaborate with experts, and advance research focused on developing economies. For PhD candidates seeking international research exposure, publication opportunities, and academic networking, this programme provides a strong platform to enhance both dissertation quality and career development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Visiting PhD fellows typically spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution. During the programme, fellows prepare one or more research papers and present a seminar on their research findings. They may also have the opportunity to publish their research in the WIDER Working Paper Series . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); You can read about previous experiences from the programme, here . About the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme enables registered doctoral students to: Use UNU-WIDER s research resources and facilities Collaborate with researchers on shared research interests Develop research papers related to their dissertation or thesis Present a seminar based on their research findings Potentially publish in the WIDER Working Paper Series ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Fellows usually spend three consecutive months at UNU-WIDER before returning to their home institution. Fellowship Duration and Research Activities During the three-month fellowship period, participants are expected to: Advance their doctoral research Engage with UNU-WIDER researchers and academic discussions Prepare research outputs suitable for publication Present their research in a seminar setting The programme is designed to strengthen research quality and support policy-relevant academic work. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be currently enrolled in a PhD programme Demonstrate ability to conduct research on developing economies Possess strong quantitative and/or qualitative analytical skills Be fluent in written and spoken English ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Although UNU-WIDER is an economics-focused institute, researchers from other social science disciplines may apply if their work aligns with development-related themes. Submissions on topics related to the UNU-WIDER work programme are particularly welcome. Priority Applicants Applications are especially encouraged from: Early-career researchers Female researchers Researchers from developing countries PhD candidates at later stages of their studies ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The programme is highly competitive. In recent years, approximately one percent of applications were successful. Financial Support When the fellowship is undertaken in Helsinki, UNU-WIDER provides: A travel grant covering travel to and from the applicant s institution or country of residence ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Medical insurance during the fellowship period A monthly stipend of EUR 2,070 to support living expenses in Helsinki The programme does not cover expenses related to dependents. Application Timeline and Deadlines UNU-WIDER accepts applications twice each year. March Application Cycle Application form opens: 1 March (00:00 UTC+3) Deadline: 31 March (23:59 UTC+3) Fellowship period: September to November (same year) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); September Application Cycle Application form opens: 1 September (00:00 UTC+3) Deadline: 30 September (23:59 UTC+3) Fellowship period: March to June (following year) The online application link becomes active one month before each deadline. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed online application form Updated curriculum vitae (CV) Reference letter from PhD supervisor The reference letter must be sent directly by the supervisor to: phdreference@wider.unu.edu This letter also confirms the applicant s enrolment in a PhD programme. Applications submitted by email or post are not accepted. Selection Process Applications are assessed competitively based on eligibility and research alignment Final decisions are communicated by email within four weeks after the deadline Selected applicants must confirm acceptance within two weeks of notification ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Why Apply for the UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship? This fellowship is ideal for doctoral researchers who want to: Strengthen their dissertation with international research support Work closely with leading development economists Build academic and policy research networks Increase opportunities for publication Enhance future academic and professional prospects How to Apply Interested candidates should complete the online application form once it becomes available during the application window. If you are interested in participating in this programme you should complete and submit the application form. For more details on the submission and for practical information on the programme, please see our frequently asked questions page . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Contact Information If you have questions about the programme or you experience any problems with your online application, please first check our questions and answers page . If you are still experiencing problems please contact us at the following email address: phdfellow@wider.unu.edu Please note that replies may take up to five working days. The UNU-WIDER Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 is a highly competitive and valuable opportunity for doctoral students conducting research on developing economies. With financial support, access to expert researchers, and opportunities to publish, the programme provides a strong environment for advancing high-quality, policy-relevant research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE The Visiting PhD Fellowship Programme was previously known as the PhD Internship Programme. QUICK LINKS Learning Previous experiences Frequently asked questions Visiting PhD Fellowship The post Visiting PhD Fellowship 2026 at United Nations University-WIDER — Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply now for the PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School (CGS), University of Cologne
summary: If you re an ambitious researcher looking to pursue a world-class PhD in Economics , the Cologne Graduate School in Management, Economics and Social Sciences (CGS) at the University of Cologne (UoC) is one of the most competitive and rewarding graduate destinations in Europe. Why This Programme is a Top Global Choice The PhD Programme in Economics at CGS/UoC stands out because it offers: A fully English-taught, research-driven curriculum with international faculty. Rigorous training that prepares you for academic careers and top research jobs worldwide. Structured supervision and research opportunities across economics, econometrics, political economy, behavioral economics, and more. A vibrant research ecosystem with connections to major research centers like the Cluster of Excellence ECONtribute: Markets Public Policy and external partners like the Max Planck Institute. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); No tuition and multiple funding avenues including fully funded PhD positions, scholarships, and research assistant roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Funding, Scholarships Benefits 1. Full PhD Funding Fully funded doctoral positions through CGS or partners. Funding typically covers living expenses during the PhD stage. 2. Stipends for M.Sc. Stage A limited number of scholarships available for exceptional M.Sc. students. Who Can Apply: Eligibility Qualifications 1. Applicants with a Bachelor s Degree You can enter the programme via the M.Sc. Economic Research (First Stage), designed to bridge undergraduate preparation to doctoral research. Ideal if: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Your Bachelor s is in Economics, Econometrics, Finance, or related fields. You rank among the top graduates in your cohort. Outcome:Completing the M.Sc. successfully gives you direct access to the doctoral (PhD) stage. 2. Applicants with a Master s Degree Students holding a master s degree in Economics or a closely related discipline can apply directly to the PhD Stage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Strong qualification signals include: Proven research interest or thesis evidence. Academic excellence (top GPA/grades). Clear match between your research interests and faculty expertise. Additional Training or Course Work If your Master s does not include sufficient Economics coursework, you may need up to one year of additional courses drawn from the M.Sc. curriculum. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Application Checklist Submission Requirements To build a strong application, prepare the following: Standard Application Materials Certified degree transcripts and diplomas (Bachelor s/Master s) Detailed CV with academic highlights. Research proposal (ideally aligned with faculty research) Recommendation letters (academic) Motivation letter explaining your fit for research. Proof of English proficiency (if not from an English-medium degree). (site may specify requirements when applications open) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Apply here Application Deadlines Intake Periods PhD Programme (with Integrated M.Sc. Option) 15th March 2026 for PhD 31st March 2026 for Msc Programme starts every October Tip: Mark mid-January to early February in your calendar to check for open application calls. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Strategic Tips to Win Admission Start Preparing Early applications open January; deadlines are strict. Match faculty research show clear alignment between your research vision and CGS strengths. Solid research proposal boosts competitiveness in this highly selective programme. Strong academic record is essential, especially at the master s level. Other Funding Opportunities External fellowships such as EWI Fellowship for Energy Economics (requiring separate application). Some doctoral positions are offered jointly with partner research institutions (e.g Max Planck Institute). Additional Perks Extensive conference funding and research stay support. Opportunities for joint publications and collaborative research. No tuition fees only a nominal semester fee ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The PhD Programme in Economics at the Cologne Graduate School is a globally respected research destination and for good reason. With no tuition , funded PhD positions , a strong international research network , and excellent post-graduate prospects , it s a transformative step for future economists and academic leaders.
